entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor L. Moench, the crop species used in the study to investigate Striga resistance

entities.name: STRIGA ASIATICA
entities.description: A parasitic weed (Striga asiatica (L.) Kuntze) that affects sorghum crops

entities.name: STRIGA RESISTANCE
entities.description: The genetic ability of sorghum hybrids and parents to resist infestation by Striga asiatica

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the agricultural experiments were conducted

entities.name: ICRISAT ASIA CENTER
entities.description: An experimental location in Patancheru, India

entities.name: AKOLA
entities.description: An experimental location in Maharashtra State, India

entities.name: RANDOMIZED COMPLETE BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The statistical experimental design used to arrange the sorghum hybrids and parents

entities.name: STRIGA INCIDENCE
entities.description: A measured trait representing the frequency or occurrence of Striga infestation

entities.name: DAYS TO 50% FLOWERING
entities.description: A measured trait representing the time taken for 50% of the crop to flower

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: A measured trait representing the vertical growth of the sorghum plant

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: A measured trait representing the amount of grain produced per plant

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE ACTION
entities.description: A genetic mechanism where the effect of alleles adds up to determine the phenotype

entities.name: NON-ADDITIVE GENE ACTION
entities.description: A genetic mechanism involving dominance or epistasis, found important for Striga resistance

entities.name: GENETICS
entities.description: The study of the hereditary mechanisms controlling Striga resistance in sorghum

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The specific environmental timeframe (1995 rainy season) during which the study was conducted
